differences.title: 1. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Astro ships zero JavaScript to the client by default, enhancing initial load times significantly.
differences.rightDesc: Next.js hydrates components on the client, which can impact initial load performance.

differences.title: 2. ARCHITECTURE
differences.leftDesc: Astro uses Islands Architecture for selective hydration, loading components independently.
differences.rightDesc: Next.js offers flexible routing with App Router or Pages, and Server Components.

differences.title: 3. CONTENT SITES
differences.leftDesc: Astro excels for content-heavy sites like blogs and marketing pages, prioritizing speed.
differences.rightDesc: Next.js is versatile, suitable for dynamic apps and content sites.

differences.title: 4. FRAMEWORK CHOICE
differences.leftDesc: Astro supports multiple UI frameworks (React, Vue, Svelte) or no framework at all.
differences.rightDesc: Next.js is primarily built around React and its ecosystem.

differences.title: 5. SERVER SIDE
differences.leftDesc: Astro renders on the server, generating static HTML with minimal client-side JS.
differences.rightDesc: Next.js offers powerful server-side rendering, API routes, and Server Components.

differences.title: 6. DEVELOPMENT
differences.leftDesc: Astro's developer experience is streamlined for content sites and performance.
differences.rightDesc: Next.js provides a comprehensive feature set for complex application development.

differences.title: 7. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for landing pages, blogs, documentation, and e-commerce product pages.
differences.rightDesc: Best for complex SPAs, SaaS platforms, and full-stack dynamic applications.
